question_structure.context: Objects A and B, each with a mass of $m=1\mathrm{kg}$, are attached to the two ends of a massless string. The string passes over a fixed pulley at the top of an inclined plane with an angle of $\theta{=}37^{\circ}$, which is fixed on a horizontal ground. Initially, object B is pulled to the bottom of the inclined plane, while object A is at a height of $h=1\mathrm{m}$ above the ground, as shown in the figure. If the inclined plane is sufficiently long, and friction between $B$ and the inclined plane, as well as between the string and the pulley, is negligible, the system is released from rest and allowed to move. (Take $g$ as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.)
question_structure.sub_question_1: The speed of object A when it lands.
question_structure.sub_question_2: If the connection between object B and the string breaks at the moment object A hits the ground, then the time required for object B to return to the bottom of the inclined plane from this moment onward is

sub_question_1.step_1: Let the velocity of object A just before it hits the ground be $v$. According to the work-energy theorem, $mg h - mg h \sin\theta = \frac{1}{2}(m + m)v^{2}$, solving for $v$ gives $v = 2\,\mathrm{m/s}$.

sub_question_2.step_2: Let the acceleration of object B moving on the inclined plane be $a$, then $ma=mg\sin\theta$, so $a=6\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$, directed downward along the inclined plane.
sub_question_2.step_3: Let the speed of object $B$ when it returns to the bottom of the inclined plane be $v_{B}$. According to the law of conservation of mechanical energy, we have $\frac{1}{2}mv_{\mathrm{B}}^{2}=mg h+\frac{1}{2}mv^{2}$, so $v_{B}=4\mathrm{m/s}$.
sub_question_2.step_4: Taking the downward direction along the incline as the positive direction, we know that $v=-2\mathrm{m/s}$, then we have $v_{B}=v+a t$.
sub_question_2.step_5: Simultaneously solving step_2, step_3, and step_4, we obtain $t=1\mathrm{s}$.

image_captions: A diagram depicts a simple pulley system. Block A hangs vertically from a rope, while block B rests on a 37-degree inclined plane. The rope runs over a pulley and connects the two blocks. Block A is 1 meter above the ground.

result_quantity.name: The speed of object A just before it hits the ground
The acceleration of object B moving on the inclined plane

step_3.physical_theorem: Law of conservation of mechanical energy

result_quantity.name: The speed of object B when it returns to the bottom of the inclined plane

step_4.physical_theorem: Kinematic equation for uniformly accelerated motion

result_quantity.name: The initial velocity of object B when it starts upward

step_5.physical_theorem: Kinematic equation for uniformly accelerated motion and Solving simultaneous equations

result_quantity.name: The time required for object B to return to the bottom of the inclined plane
